Risk_Statement: Privileged accounts in VLAN 40 have no periodic review — if credentials are compromised or misused there is no mechanism to detect or revoke access in a timely manner resulting in potential data breach

Third-party vendors are onboarded without formal security assessments — a compromised vendor with access to AceFinance systems or data could result in a supply chain breach with no prior visibility of the risk

No deprovisioning SLA exists for departing employees — former staff may retain active credentials and system access beyond their last working day creating insider threat and unauthorized access exposure
No MFA mandate or formal password policy is enforced — accounts are protected only by passwords which are susceptible to brute force phishing and credential stuffing attacks
MFA is not enforced for privileged or remote access accounts — if credentials are stolen attackers gain full access to critical systems without any secondary verification

No formal incident response plan exists — in the event of a security incident the organization has no documented process for detection containment eradication or recovery resulting in extended breach duration and regulatory exposure
No formal access control policy document exists despite VLAN segmentation being in place — without policy documentation controls cannot be consistently enforced audited or communicated to staff
Access rights are provisioned ad hoc with no periodic recertification — over-privileged accounts accumulate over time increasing the blast radius of a potential compromise
Information classification is inconsistently applied across the asset inventory — without consistent labelling sensitive data may be handled inadequately or shared without appropriate controls

Vendor contracts lack standard security and data protection clauses — in the event of a breach AceFinance has no contractual basis for requiring notification remediation or audit rights from the vendor
No triage criteria exist for security events — analysts cannot consistently distinguish a security incident from normal activity resulting in delayed or missed escalation
No containment or recovery runbooks exist — when an incident occurs response actions are improvised rather than procedural increasing recovery time and risk of incomplete remediation
SaaS tools are used without a formal cloud security policy — data residency data handling and access controls for cloud-hosted data are not formally defined or governed
No branch protection or code review gates are enforced on GitHub repositories — developers can push directly to main branches without review creating risk of malicious or erroneous code deployment
No offboarding asset-return checklist exists — departing employees may retain company devices containing sensitive data
No data transfer policy governs email and file sharing — sensitive data may be transmitted via unencrypted or unapproved channels without detection
Sub-processors used by critical vendors are not disclosed or assessed — a breach at an undisclosed sub-processor could impact AceFinance data without any prior visibility
No annual reassessment of vendor security posture is conducted — a vendor that was compliant at onboarding may have degraded controls or new exposures that remain undetected
No acceptable use policy has been distributed to staff — employees have no formal guidance on permitted use of company assets and data
Existing firewall ruleset enforces access restrictions but is not documented as a formal control — without documentation the control cannot be consistently audited or maintained
No post-incident review process exists — lessons learned from security incidents are not captured or applied resulting in repeated vulnerabilities
No evidence handling or chain-of-custody procedure exists — forensic evidence collected during an incident may be inadmissible or contaminated
No information labelling scheme has been implemented — staff cannot identify data sensitivity from the asset itself
Asset inventory exists but has no formal owner or refresh cadence — the inventory may become stale and inaccurate over time
